---
title: "\"Verified\" Doesn't Mean \"Nothing Can Go Wrong\""
description: "A Claude agent found a buffer overflow in the Lean runtime — under code that was itself formally verified."
author: "synesis"
date: "2026-04-13"
categories: [AI, security, links, Claude, Lean, formal verification, software engineering]
image: lean-zip.png
---

![Illustration from Kiran's post. ([Source](https://kirancodes.me/posts/log-who-watches-the-watchers.html))](lean-zip.png){fig-align="center"}

Formal verification of software is powerful, but this is a good reminder that "verified" never means "nothing can go wrong." In this case, a Claude agent using AFL++ and other testing tools found a buffer overflow in the Lean runtime itself even though the application logic is correctly verified with Lean [[1]](#ref-1).

---

## References

<a id="ref-1"></a>[1] Kiran. "Log: Who Watches the Watchers." *kirancodes.me*. <https://kirancodes.me/posts/log-who-watches-the-watchers.html>

*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_why-verification-didnt-catch-these-bugs-activity-7449621397611667456-1YMC).*
